title: I Am an Investor in Training. I Am an Investor.
subtitle: An affirmation for claiming the identity before the account balance warrants it
excerpt: A two-part affirmation that moves from the transition state (learning) to the declared identity (investor), without waiting for a particular account balance to justify the claim.

content.text: A two-part affirmation that names both where you are (learning) and what you already are (an investor). The two parts aren't contradictory. You don't have to finish learning before the identity applies.
I am an investor in training. I am an investor.

Say it three times in the morning, once at the start of any session where you're reading about or making investment decisions. The first sentence acknowledges the learning; the second doesn't make you wait for the end of it. Both are present tense because both are true right now.
